description: withPaymentLock uses try/finally to guarantee lock release even on error; withAuditSpan and withTraceSpan call begin/end straight-line and leak the span on exception (acceptable for observability).

findings.rationale: withPaymentLock wraps fn() in try/finally with release in the cleanup path; the other two wrappers don't. The finally is load-bearing — a thrown error from fn() leaves the Postgres advisory lock held until connection death. extra-try shape, on critical path.
